title: PR #3: Update Studio button to green

lessons.title: PR Overview
lessons.guide:
## PR #3: Update Studio button to green

**Task:** KAN-102 · **Repo:** annotate · **Branch:** feature/green-studio-button

### What this PR does

A simple visual change — updates the Studio button on the Annotate landing page from gray to green (emerald-600).

### Why

The Studio button was visually indistinct from the rest of the page. Making it green gives it more prominence as a call-to-action, signalling that it's an interactive tool rather than just another navigation link.

### Files changed

| File | What changed |
|------|-------------|
| `src/components/ModuleLanding.tsx` | Changed button Tailwind classes from gray to emerald |

### Impact

Purely cosmetic — no logic changes, no new dependencies.

lessons.title: ModuleLanding.tsx
lessons.guide:
## src/components/ModuleLanding.tsx

The landing page component that displays the module grid and the Studio button.

### What changed

One line — the Tailwind CSS classes on the Studio `<a>` tag:

**Before:**
```
bg-gray-800 hover:bg-gray-700 text-gray-300 border-gray-700
```

**After:**
```
bg-emerald-600 hover:bg-emerald-500 text-white border-emerald-500
```

The green-highlighted line 82 shows the updated classes. The button now uses Tailwind's `emerald` colour scale:
- `bg-emerald-600` — solid green background
- `hover:bg-emerald-500` — slightly lighter on hover
- `text-white` — white text (was gray-300)
- `border-emerald-500` — matching green border

### Design note

Emerald was chosen over plain green because it's softer and fits better with the dark theme. The 600/500 shade pair gives a subtle hover effect without being too flashy.
